svg.title: ApplyTrackr — GitHub Actions limitations vs Temporal solution
svg.desc: Side-by-side comparison of three root problems with GitHub Actions against how Temporal solves each.
svg.text: Root problem 1 — No durable state between steps
3 separate runs. Labels are the database.
No resume if a run fails mid-way.
Root problem 2 — No reliable event delivery

g.text: issues:closed silently dropped · no error · no log
CI never re-triggers · PR hangs indefinitely

svg.text: Both found in production. Fix: GH_PAT swap everywhere.
Root problem 3 — Eventually-consistent APIs that lie
Timing hacks on top of a lying API.

g.text: long-poll via signals · no sleep loops
dedup trivial — one workflow per issue

svg.text: The workaround tax — 20+ symptoms, same 3 root problems

g.text: GH_PAT swap on every merge and push
AC verify capped at 2 attempts, no rollback
full audit trail in Temporal UI

svg.text: Migration scope — feature pipeline only · bug fix flows stay on GitHub Actions

g.text: bug fix · CI repair · deploy fix · nightly E2E
